hypotheses_tested.summary: Probe only the anchor-12 terminal-y micro-ridge around the current leader. No corridor grafting, no basis change, just lighter / heavier / directionally biased reclosure on the leader backbone.
Probe only tiny l9 balance changes around the companion ridge, keeping the same l8 entry conditioner and unified core, with no extra corridor structure.

Exactly one structurally justified bridge between leader and companion: slightly soften the leader-side l9 while slightly lightening the terminal l12 reclosure. This is the only true hybrid in the batch.

rows_sorted.rationale: Leader-only directional split with preserved total terminal dwell (1.0 s total): front-load more hold on the outgoing l12 leg (0.625 / 0.375) to test whether the useful terminal signal is concentrated before exact closure.
Leader-only heavier terminal reclosure: keep the leader backbone unchanged and raise anchor-12 y dwell from 0.5 s to 0.625 s on each leg.
Leader-only lighter terminal reclosure: keep the leader backbone unchanged and reduce anchor-12 y dwell from 0.5 s to 0.375 s on each leg.

Single tiny bridge between leader and companion: nudge l9 slightly toward the companion (1.0 -> 0.9375) while lightening terminal l12 from 0.5 -> 0.375, with no corridor grafting and no extra family changes.
Tiny companion-side tightening above 0.75 s: keep the same companion structure and move only the l9 y gate to 0.8125 s.
Tiny companion-side softening below 0.75 s: keep the same companion structure and move only the l9 y gate to 0.6875 s.

best_candidate.rationale: Leader-only directional split with preserved total terminal dwell (1.0 s total): front-load more hold on the outgoing l12 leg (0.625 / 0.375) to test whether the useful terminal signal is concentrated before exact closure.

kf36_gate_reason: Rechecked the near-leader terminal micro-ridge candidates that stayed within the Markov42 gate.

partial_tradeoff_candidates.rationale: Leader-only directional split with preserved total terminal dwell (1.0 s total): front-load more hold on the outgoing l12 leg (0.625 / 0.375) to test whether the useful terminal signal is concentrated before exact closure.
Leader-only heavier terminal reclosure: keep the leader backbone unchanged and raise anchor-12 y dwell from 0.5 s to 0.625 s on each leg.
Leader-only lighter terminal reclosure: keep the leader backbone unchanged and reduce anchor-12 y dwell from 0.5 s to 0.375 s on each leg.

Single tiny bridge between leader and companion: nudge l9 slightly toward the companion (1.0 -> 0.9375) while lightening terminal l12 from 0.5 -> 0.375, with no corridor grafting and no extra family changes.

bottom_line.statement: No probe in this next batch beat the full leader triplet. The ridge still shows partial trade-offs (some points help mean or max), but the combined leader basin around relay_l12y0p5_on_entry remains locally saturated under these continuity-safe micro-moves.
